microsoft_ft: Scaled Azure's recovery validation program from a 2-person operation into a **platformized system**, standardizing release workflows to sustain 45+ annual resilience drills and drive **$14M+ business impact** across enterprise customers including Databricks, Walmart, SAP, and NetApp.

Led 0→1 development of an internal Resilience Automation Platform, defining program requirements and self-service scheduling capabilities that reduced operational toil by **30%** and transitioned test execution to a scalable, repeatable delivery model across multiple product lines.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive resilience testing capability, delivering a rack-level drill program in **4 months** at a 94% recovery rate — surfacing critical hardware defects and establishing a continuous V&V model that became the standard for future release validation.

Directed **14 cross-org recovery executions** under executive visibility, coordinating dependencies across SW, Systems, and Infrastructure teams — including serving as bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud network isolation test tied to a $1.5B+ enterprise contract.

Drove strategic stakeholder alignment by communicating program risks, test status, and milestone health to senior leadership on a regular cadence, enabling data-driven decisions across concurrent high-priority software delivery tracks.

Scaled team leverage through **AI-driven automation**, building an internal AI agent and restructuring planning workflows to reduce program planning cycle time by 39% and increase drill execution capacity by 21% without adding headcount.
microsoft_2023: Championed **product adoption** for AI-driven code generation workflows, conducting user demos and structured training sessions that drove active utilization across 14 key Azure engineering teams and saved **37 engineering hours** monthly in development toil.

Influenced the program roadmap to prioritize intent-based YAML generation by facilitating **11+ user interviews** with Azure service teams, surfacing critical feature gaps and translating findings into scoped, actionable backlog items aligned to release schedules.

Improved data integrity and test lookup efficiency by implementing rigorous metadata standards and migrating documentation to an AI-powered semantic search tool, **cutting retrieval time by 83%** and reducing friction in cross-team V&V workflows.
microsoft_2022: Generated **$3M in accelerated revenue** and launched Azure regions 28% faster by securing cross-functional alignment on a unified automation prioritization framework, coordinating scope and delivery dependencies across 140+ engineering and operations teams.

Mobilized a program strategy to eliminate manual toil during region launches — conducting structured discovery with 20+ service teams to identify **81 hours of manual effort per region** and building a delivery roadmap to automate the highest-risk critical paths.

Enabled leadership to identify automation gaps and prioritize high-impact test and operations work by engineering a **Power BI dashboard** tracking toil metrics and milestone status across 140+ teams, turning raw data into actionable program visibility.
amazon_robotics: Achieved **zero operational downtime** during a 2,000+ unit pilot transition by defining the legacy OS migration strategy, mapping software and hardware dependencies across 1,200+ stations, and coordinating release readiness across cross-functional engineering teams.

Facilitated Agile ceremonies — including sprint planning, backlog grooming, and retrospectives — **resolving 40+ high-priority tickets** and stabilizing the developer intake process to keep the program on schedule against aggressive delivery milestones.

Drove cross-functional alignment between IT, Operations, and Engineering to implement **automated CI/CD pipelines**, accelerating the software deployment and test execution cycle by 25% and reducing manual release overhead across the robotics platform.
pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by optimizing end-to-end scoping and delivery workflows, managing the full proposal and invoicing lifecycle for 200+ monthly engagements through a redesigned CRM process that improved prioritization and on-time closure rates.

Reduced Customer Acquisition Cost by **13%** and boosted lead conversions by 2.7% by executing a digital-first go-to-market strategy, optimizing the company website and Google Business profile to align messaging with high-intent customer segments.

Improved leadership pricing decisions by conducting **financial profitability analysis** across 1,000+ project records, identifying margin leaks and delivering data-driven recommendations that informed scope-setting and resource allocation frameworks.

tailoring_notes: JD term 'V&V' woven into microsoft_2023 bullet#3 and amazon_robotics bullet#1 to mirror Aeva's QA/test validation language directly.
JD term 'release schedules' used in microsoft_2023 bullet#2 and 'release readiness' in amazon_robotics bullet#1 to reflect Aeva's emphasis on coordinating SW release and delivery across products.

JD term 'CI/CD' surfaced explicitly in amazon_robotics bullet#3, and 'test execution' language used in microsoft_ft bullet#2 and microsoft_2022 bullet#3 to align with Aeva's test automation and release process focus.
JD phrase 'program risks/status' mirrored verbatim in microsoft_ft bullet#5 to match Aeva's stakeholder communication requirement.

JD concept 'drive closure on technical issues and development roadblocks' echoed in microsoft_ft bullet#4 via 'coordinating dependencies' and 'cross-org recovery executions under executive visibility.'
